features.claim: Antigravity supports an open standard for extending agent capabilities via reusable skill packages stored in structured folders.
features.name: Agent Skills
features.quote: Skills are an open standard for extending agent capabilities. A skill is a folder containing a SKILL.md file with instructions that the agent can follow when working on specific tasks.

features.claim: Skills can be scoped to a single workspace, enabling project-specific workflows like team deployment processes or testing conventions.
features.name: Workspace-specific skills
features.quote: Workspace skills are great for project-specific workflows, like your team's deployment process or testing conventions.

features.claim: Skills can be defined globally to apply across all workspaces for personal utilities or general-purpose tools.
features.name: Global skills
features.quote: Global skills work across all your projects. Use these for personal utilities or general-purpose tools you want everywhere.

features.claim: The agent automatically discovers, activates, and executes skills based on conversational context without requiring explicit user instruction.
features.name: Progressive disclosure
features.quote: Skills follow a progressive disclosure pattern: 1. Discovery... 2. Activation... 3. Execution... You don't need to explicitly tell the agent to use a skill—it decides based on context.

features.claim: Skills support optional helper scripts, reference examples, and resource templates alongside the required SKILL.md instructions file.
features.name: Skill folder structure
features.quote: While SKILL.md is the only required file, you can include additional resources: scripts/ Helper scripts (optional), examples/ Reference implementations (optional), resources/ Templates and other assets (optional).
